description: A wooded clearing under thick canopy. A dirt path runs north to south. To the right of the path is a wardstone with braziers.
customIntroduction: The forest trail is older than the road that replaced it, and the trees lean close. Half a mile in you find the first wardstone exactly where Bram said it would be: a waist-high pillar of grey rock, carved with a binding-glyph you have only ever seen drawn in a book — and cracked clean through, the two halves leaning apart like a broken tooth. The air around it is cold and wrong. Something has been here recently; the leaf-litter is scuffed, and you are not alone among the trees.
customContext:
A woodland trail pushes through dense canopy, the old bark pressing in on both sides. The path opens into a ragged clearing — perhaps thirty feet across — where a waist-high wardstone pillar stands at its centre, split clean through along a diagonal fault. The binding-glyph carved into its face is ancient Cape-work, the kind that belonged to the Old Empire's circuit of perimeter stones; it glows with nothing now. The air in the clearing is a few degrees colder than it should be, and the leaf-litter around the base has been thoroughly scuffed by something that came here more than once.

The tree-line is thick on three sides. On the eastern edge the trail continues toward a gorge and a bridge. The player enters from the western trail.

Exploration phase comes first. Reed can approach and examine the stone freely before anything moves — the scavengers are hunkered in the scrub to the north-east, not yet aggressive. Two secrets reward careful searching: reading the glyph by Arcana or History (DC 12) tells her the crack is structural decay, not clean sabotage — the ward rotted from within; a Perception or Investigation check (DC 10) near the scuffed leaf-litter north of the stone picks up a trail of fresh boot- and claw-prints leading into the scrub, and at the stone's base a cached healing vial wrapped in oilcloth waits (Perception DC 12) for whoever left it — probably a Cape who never came back.

When Reed follows the track-line or presses into the north-east scrub, she disturbs the nest. The skirmishers — three goblin scavengers drawn by the failing ward's silence, and one desperate human scrounger who fell in with them — break from cover and the fight begins. They are opportunists, not soldiers; they came for salvage, not slaughter. Keep the clearing's edge in mind: a player who picks her ground before disturbing the nest earns the advantage the scene was designed to reward.

On victory, the wardstone cannot be mended here — the binding rite needs the keystone, and Reed does not have it. She can note the crack, salvage what the scavengers carried, and follow the only dry road east toward the gorge bridge.

Triggers:
1. On encounter start (T0 announcement), the scene opens with the read-aloud: the trail, the clearing, the cracked stone, the cold air — Reed arrives.
2. A DC 12 Arcana/History Perception check (T1) fires when Reed enters the stone's immediate tile — pass text confirms structural decay, not sabotage; fail text gives her only an uneasy sense the glyph is dead.
3. A DC 10 Perception/Investigation check (T2) fires when Reed steps into the disturbed leaf-litter north of the stone — pass text reveals fresh tracks leading into the north-east scrub; fail text notes only that the ground is scuffed.
4. A DC 12 Perception check (T3) fires at the stone's base — pass text uncovers a cached oilcloth-wrapped vial; fail text finds only cold mud.
5. When Reed enters the north-east scrub (T4 combat), the nest is disturbed: all four skirmishers flip hostile and combat begins. The AI GM narrates the moment the undergrowth erupts.
6. On encounter completion (T5 xp), Reed earns XP for both the exploration and the fight — enough to push her to level 2.
objective: Examine the cracked wardstone, learn what you can, and deal with whatever the failing ward has drawn in from the moor. You cannot mend the stone here — that rite needs the keystone. Note it, survive, and follow the trail east to the gorge bridge.

then.message: EXPLORATION PHASE. Reed arrives on the old forest trail. Describe the canopy closing in, the clearing opening, the cracked wardstone at its centre — the glyph dead, the air cold, the leaf-litter scuffed. Two or three sentences of atmosphere. Do NOT start combat yet. The scavengers are hunkered in the north-east scrub and have not yet shown themselves — do NOT describe them or their positions until the combat trigger fires. Invite her to look around.

onPass.message: You press your fingers to the fault-line and read the glyph carefully. This is not a clean break — no tool did this. The ward decayed from within, the binding-matrix unravelling over years, maybe decades. Whatever the Old Empire set here to keep the moor quiet, it simply… stopped. The crack is a symptom, not a wound. Whoever is responsible, it isn't sabotage.

You crouch over the scuffed litter north of the stone. Boot-prints, and something smaller — clawed, three-toed, pressed deep as if carrying weight. Recent: the mud hasn't dried. Two sets, maybe three, all leading the same direction: into the scrub to the north-east. Whatever made these came here more than once.

Wedged into a gap at the stone's base, wrapped tight in oilcloth and sealed with a Cape's wax stamp: a small glass vial, a dull red liquid inside. A healing draught, cached here by someone who meant to come back. The stamp is old — a licensing mark from three seasons ago. They didn't come back.

then.text: The undergrowth erupts — the scavengers break from cover!

then.message: COMBAT BEGINS. Three goblin scavengers and one desperate human scrounger (a bandit who fell in with them). Reed's first real fight. Narrate her nerves — the sudden noise, the tightening chest, the moment her hand finds her wand before her mind catches up. Keep the tone survivable but real. Let small successes feel large. If she casts, make it count in the narration.

ENCOUNTER COMPLETE. Reed has survived her first fight. Narrate the aftermath: the quiet returning to the clearing, the cold still hanging around the cracked stone. She cannot mend it — the rite needs the keystone. She can salvage whatever the scavengers carried. The only dry road forward runs east, toward the gorge bridge. Award the sense that something has changed in her — she has cast in anger now, and the door she knocked on did not answer. This time.
